$schema: Registry of canonical fixture scenarios. Each entry pins a byte-baseline the per-skill replay tests in src/**/*.test.ts compare against. See README.md for layout.

scenarios.description: Single-service nginx deploy on testnet; no custom domain; broadcast returns LEASE_STATE_ACTIVE with one running instance.

Single-service nginx + custom domain on testnet; create-lease tx succeeds, set-item-custom-domain tx fails; MCP error envelope carries 'Deploy partially succeeded:' message with the created lease UUID.
Single-item lease; set new FQDN; setItemCustomDomain succeeds and the post-broadcast leases-by-tenant query shows the FQDN matches the expected value (verifyAndRecover outcome=match).

Set FQDN broadcast accepted but post-broadcast chain shows a different actual customDomain; verifyAndRecover routes to the mismatch branch (inform-only); onFailure is invoked with the exact reason and the orchestrator throws TX_FAILED.
Single-item lease; clear the existing customDomain; post-broadcast verify sees empty customDomain (match against expected='').
Read-only lookup against the chain's lease_by_custom_domain query; returns the typed { leaseUuid } for the lease that owns the FQDN.
Lookup for an unclaimed FQDN; chain query rejects with NotFound; orchestrator catches and returns { lease: null }.
Stack lease (two items: web, db); set FQDN on the web item; serviceName threads through both the broadcast and the verify-spec; post-broadcast verify shows match on the web item.
Active lease closed via stopApp; post-broadcast leases-by-tenant query shows state=LEASE_STATE_CLOSED (terminal). Verifier marks success; result carries finalState=LEASE_STATE_CLOSED.
stopApp accepted but post-broadcast verify shows state=LEASE_STATE_PENDING (chain settlement delay); inform-only failure with the canonical pending_drift reason; orchestrator throws TX_FAILED.

stopApp accepted but the target lease is not visible in the tenant payload after broadcast (paging gap or wrong-tenant submission); inform-only failure with the not-in-tenant-leases reason; orchestrator throws TX_FAILED.

ACTIVE single-item lease with a custom domain. Markdown report renders chain state, provider UUID, created timestamp, items, and ACTIVE-state guidance (chain-only — provider-side status is out of scope for PR 4).
PENDING lease with no items; renderer produces '_No items found_' placeholder and the pending-state guidance bullets.
CLOSED lease with createdAt + closedAt timestamps, plus a 2-item stack body with empty custom domains; renderer emits Closed line + terminal guidance bullets.
Chain returns { lease: null } for the requested UUID; orchestrator invokes onFailure with 'Lease X not found on chain.' and throws QUERY_FAILED.
